segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: Oh, what did the judges say?

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: That it was too 'busy' as a picture.

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: So what was the theme of the second competition?
segments.zh: 那第二个比赛的主题是什么？

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: Oh, sunsets, that's a great theme.

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: I see.
segments.zh: 我明白了，所以是时机不对。

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: So the timing wasn't right.

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: Well, that's a difficult subject!

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: I know!
segments.zh: 是啊！我拍了几百张照片。

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: I had to take hundreds of shots.

segments.speaker: BREDA
segments.en: I'm sure – because animals move all the time.
segments.zh: 肯定的——因为动物一直在动。

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Thanks, that's great.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: This evening we're delighted to welcome Dan Beagle, who's just written a book on looking for and finding food in the wild.
segments.zh: 今晚我们很高兴请到丹·比格尔，他刚写了一本关于在野外寻找和发现食物的书。他将告诉我们所有关于采摘野生蘑菇需要了解的知识。

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: He's going to tell us everything we need to know about picking wild mushrooms.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Thank you very much.
segments.zh: 非常感谢。嗯，我需要先谈谈安全问题。你真的需要知道自己在做什么，因为有些蘑菇毒性极强。话虽如此，一旦你知道要找什么，为了获取商店里买不到的丰富多样的蘑菇，这真的很值得。但当然，你必须非常小心，这就是为什么我总是说永远不要吃朋友或邻居采摘的蘑菇——永远记住，有些毒蘑菇看起来和可食用的蘑菇非常相似，人们很容易混淆。另一件要避免的事情是，出于明显的原因，不要采摘生长在繁忙道路旁的蘑菇。但没有什么比得上新鲜采摘的蘑菇的味道——别忘了商店里的蘑菇往往已经放了好几天，不再新鲜了。

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Well, I need to start by talking about safety.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: You really need to know what you're doing because some mushrooms are extremely poisonous.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Having said that, once you know what to look for, it's really worth doing for the amazing variety of mushrooms available – which you can't get in the shops.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: But of course, you have to be very careful and that's why I always say you should never consume mushrooms picked by friends or neighbours – always remember that some poisonous mushrooms look very similar to edible ones and it's easy for people to get confused.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: The other thing to avoid is mushrooms growing beside busy roads for obvious reasons.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: But nothing beats the taste of freshly picked mushrooms – don't forget that the ones in the shops are often several days old and past their best.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: There are certain ideas about wild mushrooms that it's important to be aware of.
segments.zh: 关于野生蘑菇有一些重要的观念需要了解。不要听信那些告诉你只能吃颜色苍白或暗淡的蘑菇的人——这完全不对。例如，有些可食用的蘑菇是鲜红色的。就我个人而言，我更喜欢煮熟的蘑菇，但生吃沙拉也不会对你有害——没有必要削皮。另一件要记住的事情是，你不能通过气味来判断蘑菇是否可以安全食用——一些最致命的蘑菇没有气味，而且据说味道还不错。最后，仅仅因为鹿或松鼠吃某种蘑菇并不意味着你也可以吃。

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Don't listen to people who tell you that it's only OK to eat mushrooms that are pale or dull – this is completely untrue.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Some edible mushrooms are bright red, for example.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Personally, I prefer mushrooms cooked but it won't do you any harm to eat them uncooked in salads – it's not necessary to peel them.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Another thing you should remember is that you can't tell if a mushroom is safe to eat by its smell – some of the most deadly mushrooms have no smell and taste quite nice, apparently.

segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: Finally, just because deer or squirrels eat a particular mushroom doesn't mean that you can.

items.prompt: Which TWO warnings does Dan give about picking mushrooms?

pairs.note: warnings 对应 safety，都是提醒注意安全。

pairs.q: never consume mushrooms picked by friends or neighbours
pairs.p: never consume mushrooms picked by friends or neighbours
pairs.note: 原文直接出现，是 Dan 给出的一个警告。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 Dan 关于采蘑菇给出了哪两个警告，定位句开头提到 safety，然后具体列出两个警告：不要吃朋友或邻居采的蘑菇，以及避免采摘繁忙道路旁的蘑菇。

items.prompt: Which TWO ideas about wild mushrooms does Dan say are correct?

pairs.note: 原文直接出现，对应题干中关于蘑菇多样性的正确观点。
原文直接出现，说明野生蘑菇在商店买不到，对应题干中的正确观点。

paraphrase.explain: Dan提到野生蘑菇的多样性（amazing variety）和商店买不到（can't get in the shops）这两个观点，与题干中的正确观点直接对应。

items.prompt: Which TWO ideas about wild mushrooms does Dan say are correct?

pairs.note: 原文直接出现，指颜色浅或暗淡的蘑菇

pairs.q: safe to eat by its smell
pairs.p: safe to eat by its smell
pairs.note: 原文直接出现，指通过气味判断蘑菇是否可食用
原文直接出现，指动物吃蘑菇不代表人也能吃

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Dan认为关于野生蘑菇哪两个观点正确，定位句直接列出了三个错误观点：颜色浅的蘑菇才能吃、通过气味判断安全性、动物吃人也能吃。这三个观点在原文中逐字出现，因此直接匹配即可。

items.prompt: Which TWO ideas about wild mushrooms does Dan say are correct?

pairs.note: 题干中pale or dull直接出现在原文，表示颜色浅或暗淡的蘑菇

pairs.q: safe to eat by its smell
pairs.p: safe to eat by its smell
pairs.note: 题干中通过气味判断是否安全，原文直接出现
题干中动物吃蘑菇，原文直接出现

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Dan认为哪两个关于野生蘑菇的观点是正确的。定位句先否定‘只吃颜色暗淡的蘑菇’，再否定‘通过气味判断安全性’，最后否定‘动物能吃人也能吃’。因此三个选项中只有这些被否定的观点是Dan认为错误的，而正确观点需从其他未提及或肯定的内容中找。但根据题目要求，这里只标注同义替换，实际答案需从选项中选择与原文否定内容相反的选项。
